summary: Credit Purchase Double-Posting Bug Fix Verified - All 20 tests passed. The fix ensures credit purchases do NOT double-count in financial reports. Credit purchase invoices create daybook entries with is_cashbook_entry=False, and only the final payment (liability settlement) creates a cashbook entry that increases actual_cost.
passed_tests: test_01_login_works - Authentication working with founder credentials
test_03_create_credit_purchase_invoice - POST /api/finance/execution-ledger creates credit purchase with liability
test_04_verify_actual_cost_unchanged_after_credit_purchase - CRITICAL: actual_cost did NOT increase after credit purchase
test_06_verify_liability_record_exists - Liability record created with status='open'
test_08_verify_actual_cost_increased_after_payment - CRITICAL: actual_cost increased ONLY after payment
test_01_project_finance_transactions_exclude_credit_purchases - Project finance excludes credit purchase entries

bug_fix_verification.issue: Credit purchases were being double-counted in financial reports - once when the purchase invoice was created and again when payment was made
bug_fix_verification.root_cause: Credit purchase invoices were creating cashbook entries that counted towards actual_cost, and then the liability settlement also created a cashbook entry
fix_applied: Credit purchases now set is_cashbook_entry=False in the daybook entry (server.py lines 31321-31356)
get_project_finance_detail excludes is_cashbook_entry=False entries from actual_cost calculation (lines 21607-21740)
get_daily_summary excludes is_cashbook_entry=False entries from daybook totals (lines 21233-21242)
settle_liability creates cashbook entry with is_cashbook_entry=True (line 26228)
evidence: Created credit purchase of 15000 - actual_cost remained unchanged
remaining_liability increased by 15000 after credit purchase
remaining_liability returned to initial value after settlement
Cashbook transactions do not contain credit purchase entries
Project finance transactions do not contain credit purchase entries

critical_code_review_comments: Fix correctly implemented - credit purchases create daybook-only entries with is_cashbook_entry=False
Query filters in get_project_finance_detail and get_daily_summary correctly exclude non-cashbook entries
Liability settlement correctly creates cashbook entry with is_cashbook_entry=True
The fix ensures vendor liabilities net to zero after payment (liability created on purchase, reduced on payment)
seed_data_creation: Created test credit purchase invoice TEST-INV-* and settled liability during testing on project proj_4aaba062
context_for_next_testing_agent: Credit purchase double-posting bug fix verified. Key test file: test_credit_purchase_double_posting_fix.py. The fix ensures: 1) Credit purchases set is_cashbook_entry=False, 2) Only liability settlements create cashbook entries, 3) actual_cost only increases on payment, not on credit purchase creation.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: Credit purchase invoices were creating accounting_transactions entries that were being counted in actual_cost calculations. When the liability was later settled, another transaction was created, resulting in double-counting.
rca_of_the_issue.impact: Financial reports showed inflated actual_cost values - purchases were counted twice (once on invoice creation, once on payment)
rca_of_the_issue.fix: Added is_cashbook_entry=False flag to credit purchase daybook entries. Modified queries in get_project_finance_detail and get_daily_summary to exclude entries with is_cashbook_entry=False. Liability settlement explicitly sets is_cashbook_entry=True.
rca_of_the_issue.verification: All 20 tests pass - credit purchase creation does not increase actual_cost, only liability settlement increases actual_cost, cashbook correctly excludes credit purchase entries
